You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/components/common/TypeaheadSelect/TypeaheadMenuToggle.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 4 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 140
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: plain

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Code context:
```
  139                icon={<TimesIcon />}
  140                variant={ButtonVariant.plain}
  141                onClick={handleClear}

```

### Fix 2
Line: 138
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: TextInputGroupUtilities

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  137            <TextInputGroupUtilities>
  138              <Button
  139                icon={<TimesIcon />}

```

### Fix 3
Line: 101
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>

Incident context:
  componentName: MenuToggle
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: __ComponentReturn__

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: MenuToggle
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  100    return (
  101      <MenuToggle
  102        ref={toggleRef}

```

### Fix 4
Line: 119
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-textinputgroupmain]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <TextInputGroupMain> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <TextInputGroupMain> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <TextInputGroupMain><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</TextInputGroupMain>
  After:  <TextInputGroupMain icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</TextInputGroupMain>

Incident context:
  componentName: TextInputGroupMain
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: TextInputGroup

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: TextInputGroupMain
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  118        <TextInputGroup isPlain>
  119          <TextInputGroupMain
  120            value={displayValue}

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/forklift-console-plugin/source/src/components/common/TypeaheadSelect/TypeaheadMenuToggle.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
Let me start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1 (Line 140): Button.variant type changed
The `variant` prop on `Button` now supports a `'stateful'` value in addition to the existing ones. The current code uses `ButtonVariant.plain` which is still valid. **No change needed** — `plain` is still in the new type.

### Fix 2 (Line 138): Icon children to prop for Button
The rule says icon content should be passed via the `icon` prop instead of as children. Looking at lines 138-143:
```tsx
<Button
  icon={<TimesIcon />}
  variant={ButtonVariant.plain}
  onClick={handleClear}
  aria-label="Clear selection"
/>
```
The `Button` already uses the `icon` prop (`icon={<TimesIcon />}`) and has no icon children — it's already self-closing. **No change needed** — this was already migrated.

### Fix 3 (Line 101): Icon children to prop for MenuToggle
Looking at the `MenuToggle` (lines 101-147), it doesn't have any icon as a direct child. Its children are a `<TextInputGroup>`. The `variant="typeahead"` means it's a typeahead toggle, not an icon toggle. **No change needed** — there's no icon child to move.

### Fix 4 (Line 119): Icon children to prop for TextInputGroupMain
Looking at `TextInputGroupMain` (lines 119-135), it's a self-closing element with no children — it has no icon children to move. **No change needed** — there's no icon child to move.

All four fixes either don't apply (the code doesn't have the pattern to fix) or the fix was already applied in a previous pass. The file is already correct.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): **No change needed** — The value `ButtonVariant.plain` is still valid in the new type union. The type change only adds `'stateful'` as a new option.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to prop for Button): **Already applied** — The `<Button>` already uses the `icon` prop (`icon={<TimesIcon />}`) and is self-closing with no icon children.

3. **Fix 3** (icon children to prop for MenuToggle): **No change needed** — The `<MenuToggle>` has no icon as a direct child; its children are `<TextInputGroup>` content (typeahead variant).

4. **Fix 4** (icon children to prop for TextInputGroupMain): **No change needed** — The `<TextInputGroupMain>` is a self-closing element with no children, so there's no icon to move.
